perturbation.original_text: Subject to paragraph 3 below, the term “Confidential Information” shall mean any and all materials or infor mation not in the public domain that either party receives or acquires from the other party in connection with any activities related thereto, and whether disclosed or made available prior to the date of thisagreement and whether in writing, electronically, orally, visually or otherwise and any notes,summaries or other materials in whatever medium or format (“Notes”) created by theReceiving Party and derived from such information or materials. Confidential Informationincludes, without limitation, (i) software, utilities, solutions, designs, techniques, methods,methodologies, tools, processes, templates, data and any information related thereto,existing or contemplated products or services, specifications and plans, (ii) forecasts,business plans, strategies, and financial statements, records and information, (iii) customerlists or requirements, and (iv) other business or technical information or trade secrets.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to paragraph 3 below, the term “Confidential Information” shall mean information clearly marked as confidential. The provisions of this Agreement shall apply to any Confidential Information that a ReceivingParty receives or becomes privy to in connection with this Agreement, on or after theEffective Date of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the detailed list of what constitutes "Confidential Information" and replacing it with a vague definition that requires information to be "clearly marked as confidential," creates an in-text contradiction because later sections of the agreement may reference items (software, business plans etc.) as if they are automatically considered confidential, even if they are not explicitly marked as such. In Section 5 the term Confidential Information is being used, after the definition of confidential information was changed, therefore creating the in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All materials and Confidential Infor mation shall remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party. Except as expressly provided herein or under a separate written agreement between the parties that references this Agreement, either party shall not be obligated to grant, convey or transfer to the other any interest, license or other right, or under its Confidential Information or any patent, copyright, trade secret, trademark or other intellectual property right.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information' broadly, including software, business plans, etc. The changed text narrows the definition to only information 'clearly marked as confidential.' The contradicted text states that 'Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party.' This creates a contradiction because under the changed definition, if the Disclosing Party does not clearly mark something as confidential, even something like their software or business plan, it is no longer considered confidential information and does not remain their exclusive property, contradicting the contradicted text. Therefore, the change breaks the compatibility of obligations.

perturbation.original_text: A Receiving Party agrees, acknowledges and undertakes to (i) hold the ConfidentialInformation of the Disclosing Party in strictest confidence, and subject to the terms ofthis Agreement, not to disclose Confidential Information to any third party unless it is aregulated investment body, solicitor, accountant or private investors known to the Party.Such information shall be shared freely. Where the Party does not know the intended thirdparty then the Party shall ask for prior written consent of the Disclosing Party (which maybe given on such terms as the Disclosing Party considers appropriate), (ii) not to use theConfidential Information for any purpose (including but not limited to any competitive or Tabun Kitchen 151 Copse Hill, London, SW20 0SU2commercial purpose) other than in relation to the PR activities as contemplated under any other written agreement between the parties which references this Agreement, and (iii) protect such Confidential Information by using the same degree of care as it uses to protect its own highly Confidential Information of similar value and sensitivity, but not less than reasonable care.
perturbation.changed_text: A Receiving Party agrees to hold the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party in reasonable confidence and to protect such Confidential Information. Where the Party does not know the intended thirdparty then the Party shall ask for prior written consent of the Disclosing Party (which maybe given on such terms as the Disclosing Party considers appropriate).
perturbation.explanation: By changing "strictest confidence" to "reasonable confidence" and removing the specific exceptions for disclosure to regulated bodies and investors and the part of usage of the confidential information, an in-text contradiction is created. Section 9 discusses remedies for a violation of the agreement, but the change to 'reasonable confidence' makes it less clear what constitutes a violation, thus weakening the remedies available and creating uncertainty in enforcement, as well as what means a violation of the confidential information usage.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agr eement, including, without limitation, the performance and enforceability hereof, shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of England & Wales. Each Receiving Party acknowledges that a violation of this Agreement by the Receiving Party may cause irreparable harm to the Disclosing Party and that the Disclosing Party may have no adequate remedy at law. Accordingly, each party agrees that the Disclosing Party shall have the right, in addition to any other rights and Tabun Kitchen 151 Copse Hill, London, SW20 0SU3 remedies it may have, at law, in equity or otherwise, to seek injunctive relief or similar such orders in any court of competent jurisdiction directing or prohibiting certain acts which may be necessary to protect the Confidential Information and the Disclosing Party. This Clause 9 shall not apply for the mere evaluation of non-patented, non-copyrighted information such as Business Plans, Business Proposals etc that have no quantifiable valuation nor unique intellectual value. This Clause 9 shall apply to valuable contacts intr oduced by either Party with regards to investors, bankers, financiers and other professionals.
perturbation.justification: The original text commits the Receiving Party to the "strictest confidence" and outlines specific exceptions for disclosure and usage. The changed text weakens this to "reasonable confidence" and removes the exceptions and usage guidelines. This creates a contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text,' which states that a violation of the agreement can cause irreparable harm and allows the Disclosing Party to seek injunctive relief. The change to "reasonable confidence" makes it unclear what constitutes a violation, weakening the enforceability of the agreement and the availability of remedies, as outlined in Section 9, thus creating a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the provisions of this Agreement, the obligations and restrictions set forth herein regarding Confidential Information shall not apply to information that the Receiving Party can establish (i) is or becomes publicly available other than as a result of a breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party, (ii) is lawfully received from a third party which is, to the reasonable knowledge and belief of the Receiving Party, not under any obligation of confidentiality for the benefit of the Disclosing Party, (iii) was either in the possession of or known to the Receiving Party at the time of disclosure without any limitation on use or disclosure for the benefit of the Receiving Party, or (iv) is independently developed by the Receiving Party without the use, r eference to or benefit of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the provisions of this Agreement, the obligations and restrictions set forth herein regarding Confidential Information shall not apply to information that the Receiving Party can establish (i) is or becomes publicly available.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the exceptions related to information lawfully received from a third party, information already possessed by the receiving party, and independently developed information. This change creates an in-text contradiction with Section 2. Now the receiving party may violate section 2. by using information from a third party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties anticipate that in connection with, any of the Parties may receive or become privy to the Confidential Infor mation of the other Parties. A Receiving Party agrees to hold the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party in reasonable confidence and to protect such Confidential Information. Where the Party does not know the intended thirdparty then the Party shall ask for prior written consent of the Disclosing Party (which maybe given on such terms as the Disclosing Party considers appropriate).
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes exceptions to the confidentiality obligations, which were present in the original text. Specifically, it eliminates exceptions for information lawfully received from a third party, information already possessed by the receiving party, and independently developed information. The contradicted text states that a receiving party agrees to hold the confidential information of the disclosing party in reasonable confidence and to protect such Confidential Information and requires prior written consent of the Disclosing Party when the Receiving Party does not know the intended third party. By removing the exception related to third-party information, the changed text now contradicts the contradicted text, because the Receiving Party may violate section 2. by using information from a third party without the Disclosing Party's prior written consent. Thus, there is a direct contradiction making the answer YES.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the earlier of (i) the completion or (ii) the Disclosing Party’s written r equest, the Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy all Confidential Information disclosed or made available by the Disclosing Party, in any form and including, all Notes, save to the extent that regulation, applicable rule, law or record retention policy requires retention of such material. Upon request, the Receiving Party shall certify in writing that it has returned or destroyed all Confidential Information in accordance with this paragraph 6, disclosed or made available by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the Disclosing Party’s written request, the Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy all Confidential Information disclosed or made available by the Disclosing Party, save to the extent that regulation, applicable rule, law or record retention policy requires retention of such material.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the "completion of the project" as a trigger for returning confidential information creates a contradiction because the agreement no longer explicitly addresses what happens to confidential information once the project is completed, potentially conflicting with the expectation of continued confidentiality implied in other sections, like remedies in section 9.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agr eement, including, without limitation, the performance and enforceability hereof, shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of England & Wales. Each Receiving Party acknowledges that a violation of this Agreement by the Receiving Party may cause irreparable harm to the Disclosing Party and that the Disclosing Party may have no adequate remedy at law. Accordingly, each party agrees that the Disclosing Party shall have the right, in addition to any other rights and Tabun Kitchen 151 Copse Hill, London, SW20 0SU3 remedies it may have, at law, in equity or otherwise, to seek injunctive relief or similar such orders in any court of competent jurisdiction directing or prohibiting certain acts which may be necessary to protect the Confidential Information and the Disclosing Party. This Clause 9 shall not apply for the mere evaluation of non-patented, non-copyrighted information such as Business Plans, Business Proposals etc that have no quantifiable valuation nor unique intellectual value. This Clause 9 shall apply to valuable contacts intr oduced by either Party with regards to investors, bankers, financiers and other professionals.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified two triggers for returning confidential information: project completion or a written request. The changed text removes 'completion of the project' as a trigger. The contradicted text (Section 9) refers to protecting confidential information. By removing project completion as a trigger, the changed text implies that confidential information might not need to be returned after the project ends, which weakens the protection of confidential information that section 9 aims to provide. This creates a contradiction because the initial agreement clearly stated an earlier return condition. The removal impacts the remedies available in section 9, making the agreement inconsistent, because if data isn't returned after completion, the violation described in 9 may not occur.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the entire understanding and agreement of the parties relating to the subject matter hereof. No provision of this Agreement may be amended, modified or waived except in writing signed by the party sought to be bound. No custom or course of dealing shall cause a modification of this Agr eement. The paragraph headings used herein are for convenience of reference only and will not affect the interpretation or construction of this Agreement. The failure of any party to enforce any provision of this Agreement shall not constitute a waiver of any rights or remedies available to such party or its right to subsequently enforce such provision or any other provision of this Agreement. Each party is responsible for their own costs and expenses in relation to fulfilling the aim and intention of this agreement and any meetings arising thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the entire understanding and agreement of the parties relating to the subject matter hereof. No provision of this Agreement may be amended, modified or waived except in writing signed by the party sought to be bound. The paragraph headings used herein are for convenience of reference only and will not affect the interpretation or construction of this Agreement. The failure of any party to enforce any provision of this Agreement shall not constitute a waiver of any rights or remedies available to such party or its right to subsequently enforce such provision or any other provision of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the sentence "No custom or course of dealing shall cause a modification of this Agreement," the modified text creates an in-text contradiction. Because the clause stating that “No custom or course of dealing” can modify the agreement has been removed, it could be inferred that custom can modify the agreement. This affects the "Entire Understanding" by making it unclear what that means because now an outside factor such as custom can modify this understanding.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement contains the entire understanding and agreement of the parties relating to the subject matter hereof. No provision of this Agreement may be amended, modified or waived except in writing signed by the party sought to be bound. The paragraph headings used herein are for convenience of reference only and will not affect the interpretation or construction of this Agreement. The failure of any party to enforce any provision of this Agreement shall not constitute a waiver of any rights or remedies available to such party or its right to subsequently enforce such provision or any other provision of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that 'No custom or course of dealing shall cause a modification of this Agreement.' This sentence is removed in the changed text. As per the explanation provided, this creates an in-text contradiction because the 'Entire Understanding' can now be modified by outside factors such as custom, which introduces ambiguity with the contradicted text which is the original agreement and all of its clauses. This aligns with the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: No Party to this Agr eement may directly or indirectly circumvent, compete with, interfere with, avoid, by-pass or obviate the interests of, or cause, support, or entice any other person(s) to circumvent, compete with, interfere with, avoid, by-pass or obviate the interests of, the other Party to this Agreement by entering into any arrangement with persons or entities introduced to that Party by the other Party in connection with the Opportunity, without first obtaining the written consent of the other Party. 11.13. Clause 11.1 shall apply to all ventur es, projects, and collaborations between the Parties. 11.14. All parties agr ee to work in the spirit of cooperation. Neither party shall compete with the other for introductions made and respect each other’s contacts. This Agreement shall be binding upon the parties and their respective successors.
perturbation.changed_text: All parties agr ee to work in the spirit of cooperation. This Agreement shall be binding upon the parties and their respective successors.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the entire Non-Circumvention and Non-Competition clause creates an in-text contradiction with the remedies clause (Section 9) because that section references the ability to seek injunctive relief for violations of the agreement. Without the non-compete clause, it’s unclear what specific actions could trigger such relief in relation to competition or circumvention. This contradicts the remedy section that ensures protections from harm.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agr eement, including, without limitation, the performance and enforceability hereof, shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of England & Wales. Each Receiving Party acknowledges that a violation of this Agreement by the Receiving Party may cause irreparable harm to the Disclosing Party and that the Disclosing Party may have no adequate remedy at law. Accordingly, each party agrees that the Disclosing Party shall have the right, in addition to any other rights and Tabun Kitchen 151 Copse Hill, London, SW20 0SU3 remedies it may have, at law, in equity or otherwise, to seek injunctive relief or similar such orders in any court of competent jurisdiction directing or prohibiting certain acts which may be necessary to protect the Confidential Information and the Disclosing Party. This Clause 9 shall not apply for the mere evaluation of non-patented, non-copyrighted information such as Business Plans, Business Proposals etc that have no quantifiable valuation nor unique intellectual value. This Clause 9 shall apply to valuable contacts intr oduced by either Party with regards to investors, bankers, financiers and other professionals.
perturbation.justification: The original text included a non-circumvention and non-competition clause. The changed text removes this clause entirely. The contradicted text (Section 9) provides remedies, including injunctive relief, for violations of the agreement. With the non-compete clause removed, it's unclear what actions related to competition or circumvention would trigger such relief, directly contradicting the stated purpose and availability of remedies in Section 9. Therefore, the change creates a strong in-text contradiction.
